rulings.Case Title: THIEN TRINH VS. NUALA THOMASON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, November 13, 2014, Line 4, DEFENDANT N.A.D., INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion for summary judgment is denied. Assuming that the broad language of the release applies to N.A.D., Inc., plaintiff establishes mutual mistake pursuant to the declaration of Mr. Do. That declaration is uncontroverted and clearly shows that the only claims to be released related to Hertz Corporation. N.A.D. Inc. was not expressly named in the release, N.A.D., Inc. and its insurer were not part of the negotiation process regarding the release, and there is no evidence that the contracting parties intended to include it as a third party beneficiary. See Hess v. Ford Motor Co., (2002) 27 Cal.4th 516, 527-531. The facts of Lama v. Comcast Cablevision (1993) 14 Cal.App.4th 59, are distinguishable. There, the party relying on the release presented evidence showing that the contracting parties intended to release all potential tortfeasors. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: DIANA D. SAM VS. CATALINA BOYKIN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Answers At The Deposition Of Cross-Defendant Myron Moskovitz, For An Order Compelling A Second Deposition Of Defendant Cross-Defendant Myron Moskovitz, And For Sanctions

The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: LESLIE FINLEY VS. DREXEL A. BRADSHAW et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Recuse Counsel Of Record For Plaintiff Leslie Finley
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, November 13, 2014, Line 16, DEFENDANTS DREXEL BRADSHAW, BRADSHAW AND ASSOCIATES, P.C.'S Motion To Recuse Counsel Of Record For Plaintiff Leslie Finley. Motion denied. California Rule of Professional Conduct 5-210 provides that an attorney may act as an advocate before a jury which will hear testimony from the attorney if the client has provided written consent to the representation. The Court may still order withdrawal of counsel in instances where an attorney ought to testify on behalf of his client, but not without "a convincing demonstration of detriment to the opponent or injury to the integrity of the judicial process." (Smith, Smith & Kring v. Sup. Ct. (1997) 60 Cal.App.4th 573, 579.) Defendant here has not shown that any detriment will be incurred by Defendant as a result of Plaintiff's counsel providing testimony at trial. Defendant also has not shown prejudice based on Plaintiff's counsel testifying as a witness because if counsel's opinion is based on privileged information, Plaintiff has the right to waive that privilege, rending the information discoverable. Nor does Defendant's conclusory statement that Defendant possesses viable claims for indemnity against Berko amount to convincing evidence. Defendant failed to offer even a scintilla of evidence relating to any misconduct or wrongdoing on the part of Plaintiff's counsel that would permit Defendant to name counsel as a defendant in a cross-complaint, and such a veiled threat is not a basis for disqualification. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: CAPITAL ONE BANK (USA) N.A. VS. WANDA MEDINA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, November 13, 2014, Line 7, PLAINTIFF CAPITAL ONE BANK (USA) N.A.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion for summary judgment is granted. Plaintiff establishes that defendant owes $3,546.24 on the account. The declaration of Barbara Horan is admissible. See Evid. 1271. In opposition, defendant fails to create a triable issue of material fact. Defendant does not provide a declaration in opposition. Defendant cannot rely on her unverified interrogatory answers to create a triable issue of material fact. See CCP 2030.410 [use of interrogatories]. The court denies defendant's request for a continuance. Defendant did not act diligently and should have sought to resolve the issue regarding Ms. Horan's deposition months ago. CCP 437c(e) provides that summary judgment should not be denied for want of cross-examination. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CCP 437c(g) with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: S. LOUIS MARTIN VS. GOOGLE, INC
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, November 13, 2014, Line 10, DEFENDANT GOOGLE, INC'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Motion granted. Defendant has met its burden of showing that the claims asserted against it arise from constitutionally protected activity, thereby shifting the burden to Plaintiff to demonstrate a probability of prevailing on the merits of the Complaint. Plaintiff has failed to file an opposition to Defendant's Motion, and has produced no evidence supporting a probability of success. rulings.Case Title: INS COMM STATE OF CA VS WESTERN EMPLOYER
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Approval Of Reinsurnce Communtation And Settlement Agreement With Xl Reinurance America Inc.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, November 13, 2014, Line 14, PETITIONER INSURANCE COMMISSIONER OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA'S Motion For Approval Of Reinsurance Commutation And Settlement Agreement With Xl Reinsurance America Inc. Granted. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The Court will execute the proposed order submitted with the moving papers. =(302/EHG)
